Positions held

2021 Assistant Professor
IPPP, Durham University, United Kingdom
2018-2021 Research associate
TRIUMF, Canada
2017 – 2018 Research associate
Dartmouth College, United States
2013 – 2017 PhD Theoretical Particle Physics
University of Sussex, United Kingdom
Advisor: Prof. Veronica Sanz

Refereed journal articles and preprints

To be found here.

Conference/graduate school organization

  • Pollica workshop on Gravitational Waves, September 2024
  • Dark Matter Beyond the Weak Scale, IPPP Duhram, March 2024
  • What’s the matter?, Mainz Institute for Theoretical Physics, August/September 2022
  • YETI (graduate school), July 2022
  • UK HEP forum, November 2021
  • Planck 2021, June 2021
  • Inaugural LISA Canada workshop, April 2021
  • A Rainbow of Dark Sectors, Aspen Centre for Phyiscs, March 2021
  • TRIUMF Theory workshops, 2019, 2020
  • HEP tools bootcamp, Fall 2017

Teaching

  • Invisibles school 2023: Lectures on Phase Transitions in the early Universe
  • NExT school 2022: Lectures on Early Universe Cosmology
  • TASI 2022: Lectures on Baryogenesis, Gravitational Waves, and Phase Transitions
  • Fall 2021: Introduction to Field Theory, part of the M.Sc. in Particles, Strings and Cosmology 
  • Summer 2019: Gravitational Waves (lecture 1 and lecture 2), at the TRISEP summer school in Vancouver

Talks

I love giving research and outreach talks. Some of the slides for my talks are available online. Here are some examples:

  • Rethinking false vacuum decay, SLAC theory seminar (virtual, June 2021)
  • Black hole archeology using gravitational waves, Perimeter Institute for Theoretical Physics (virtual, April 2021)
  • New insights into light particles and supernovae, Light Dark World conference (virtual, December 2020)
  • Microlensing with extended dark matter structures, University of Toronto THEP seminar (virtual, September 2020)
  • Review talk on Exotic Compact Objects, Gravitational Waves for Fundamental Physics workshop in Amsterdam (November 2019)
  • Reheating confronts Leptogenesis and Strong Baryogenesis, Baryon and Lepton number Violation (BLV) conference in Madrid (October 2019)
  • Gravitational Wave Probes of Dark Matter, Next Frontiers in the Search for Dark Matter conference at the Galileo Galilei Institute in Florence (September 2019)

For a complete list of recent research talks, please contact me.

Popular articles + misc media

  • Unveiling the Universe, CERN70 event, January 2024
  • Women in physics roundtable, December 2022
  • TRIUMF Saturday Morning Lectures, Heavy Black Holes and Light Subatomic Particles, March 2021
  • KICP podcast hosted by Dr. Jose Maria Ezquiaga, audio only/audio + video
  • Symmetry Magazine, Physics at tiniest scale could explain ‘impossible’ black holes, December 2020
  • Dark matter day panel discussion, October 2020
  • New Scientist, Minder ongelijkheid in de wetenschap is niet zo simpel als we zouden willen (op-ed in Dutch, in print), October 2020
  • CERN newsletter, Beyond the Standard Model Explanations of GW190521, September 2020
  • New Scientist, Zwaartekrachtsgolven van ‘te zware’ zwarte gaten waargenomen (in Dutch), September 2020
  • Kijk, Hoe axionen het lot van sterren bepaalden (in Dutch), August 2020
  • University Affairs, How a group of physicists started a protest movement in solidarity with Black Lives Matter, July 2020
  • Particles for Justice, Strike for Black Lives, June 2020 (selected press coverage here)
  • Contemporary Art Gallery Vancouver, Art and Science in conversation, January 2020
  • Physics Today, Ann Elizabeth Nelson (obituary), December 2019
  • Gizmodo, Is there a fifth dimension? June 2019
  • Wired, Physicists condemn sexism through ‘Particles of Justice’, October 2018
  • De Volkskrant, Theoretisch natuurkundigen schrijven antiseksismebrief (in Dutch), October 2018
  • Particles of Justice, High Energy Physics Community Statement, October 2018
  • Science magazine, book review of Lost in Math (Sabine Hossenfelder), June 2018
  • The Conversation (theconversation.com), Understanding the hidden dimensions of modern physics through the arts, (republished several times, including on phys.org), September 2015
  • The Quantum Messenger, personal outreach blog, July 2015 – present
